title: The most underrated token type
content: Arcade tokens offer a different model for crypto: tokens that work like loyalty points or arcade credits, but with the openness and programmability of public blockchains. The most underrated token type Some tokens are designed to be spent. Tim Roughgarden , Eddy Lazzarin , Miles Jennings , and Scott Duke Kominers 3 4 Share In our token taxonomy , we walk through seven types of tokens, including network tokens, collectible tokens, and memecoins. Of these, the least explored and most underappreciated is the arcade token : a token with a relatively stable value within a specific software or product ecosystem, often managed by an issuer (like a company). Fundamentally, arcade tokens are the blockchain-based equivalent of assets people are already familiar with: airline miles, credit card points, in-game digital gold, and so on. What all these have in common is that they are currencies that are internal to, and support the functioning of, a marketplace economy: Frequent flyer miles and rewards points, for example, can encourage brand loyalty and be used to make flight and upgrade purchases; digital gold can let you buy or sell items in a video game. While companies have used assets like these for decades, almost all previous instances have existed on centralized databases , limiting ownership, transferability, and user choice. Arcade tokens on public blockchains differ in that they are open, interoperable, and composable, which offers a range of new market-design benefits. This post aims to answer the most common questions we’ve received about arcade tokens: what they are, what

normalizedTitle: the most underrated token type

title: Your code is fast – if you're lucky
content: Recently, while I was working on an optimized Quicksort implementation, I came across a rather interesting quirk. Modern compilers (especially Clang) optimize loops using fast, branch-free instructions - provided you use the right programming style. sort.h - a quicksort with sorting networks // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT // sort.h - Branchless Quicksort // (c) christof.kaser@gmail.com #ifndef SORT_H #define SORT_H #ifndef BLQS_CMP #define BLQS_CMP(a, b) ((a) < (b)) #endif #include #include #define min(a, b) (((a) < (b)) ? (a) : (b)) #define SMALLPART 1024 #define SWSZ 512 #define UNROLL 16 #define sort2(a, b) do { \ unsigned m = BLQS_CMP(a, b); \ BLQS_TYPE x = a; \ a = m ? a : b; \ b = m ? b : x; \ } while(0) #define sort3(a, b, c) do { \ sort2(a, b); sort2(b, c); sort2(a, b); \ } while(0) #define sort4(a, b, c, d) do { \ sort2(a, b); sort2(c, d); sort2(a, c); \ sort2(b, d); sort2(b, c); \ } while(0) #define sort5(a, b, c, d, e) do { \ sort2(b, c); sort2(d, e); sort2(b, d); \ sort2(a, c); sort2(a, d); sort2(c, e); \ sort2(a, b); sort2(c, d); sort2(b, c); \ } while(0) #define sort6(a, b, c, d, e, f) do { \ sort2(a, b); sort2(c, d); sort2(e, f); \ sort2(a, c); sort2(b, d); sort2(e, f); \ sort2(a, e); sort2(b, f); sort2(c, e); \ sort2(d, f); sort2(b, c); sort2(d, e); \ sort2(c, d); \ } while(0) #define sort7(a, b, c, d, e, f, g) do { \ sort2(a, b); sort2(c, d); sort2(a, c); \ sort2(b, d); sort2(b, c); sort2(e, f); \ sort2(e, g); sort2(f, g); sort2(a, e); \ sort2(b, f); sort2(c, g); sort2(b, e); \ sort2(d, g); sort2(c, e); sort2(b, c); \ sort2(d, f); sort2(d, e); \ } while(0)
eventKey: your-code-fast-you-lucky
normalizedTitle: your code is fast – if you re lucky
summary: Software performance depends on chance; optimizations that work in one context may fail unpredictably in another due to varying conditions.

title: 又一个酷暑考验着亚利桑那州与高温相关的死亡人数下降趋势

summary: Arizona faces another severe summer, challenging recent progress in reducing heat-related deaths. The state previously saw a decline in fatalities linked to extreme temperatures, but current conditions test whether that trend can continue. This follows ongoing concerns about climate impacts in regions with intense summer heat.

title: GPT-5.6 被曝重大 bug！硅谷大佬 Mac 被一键清空
content: OpenAI - @inza9hi - https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/lzMsA4LZwfrC39nL3EFAzg这个 DCG 大家装了吗https://github.com/Dicklesworthst https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/lzMsA4LZwfrC39nL3EFAzg 这个 DCG 大家装了吗 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard 1. wx 上的新闻，我只能信一半。 2. 我去翻看了原 po 的 twitter 。初步从评论区看起来有点「开局一张图，内容全靠编」的倾向。再观望一下 @ inza9hi 我看起来和国内营销号差不多，半真半假的那种 1. 原 PO 只描述结果现象，不描述事情经过。 2. 评论区让他拿出当时的场景，他没回答过。 3. 最近几篇推文只输出情绪，没有输出有用的信息 4. 从历史推文来看，不像是编程圈的人，更像是自媒体 这不就和国内营销号差不多嘛，而且如果真有 BUG ，这个问题不应该从国内 WX 渠道首发。我也暂时没在任何社区看到有关讨论。 我更多偏向于原 PO 使用不当，或者带点炒作的嫌疑。 自媒体的文章大部分是编的，尤其不懂技术的，编的很离谱，骗流量来的。 自媒体 be like: 安装包、应用数据分不清，app store 安装包就几百 M ，几 G 的应用数据偷换成安装包大小。 就这帖子 50w 浏览量…… @ 1874w 再使用不当，只要不是使用者下命令，AI 也不不该去全删文件。 这个事确实有炒作嫌疑 但是这个人其实还挺有名的，不至于为了炒作而罔顾事实： 之前他写过一篇 Something Big Is Happening ，到处都在讨论。 @ inza9hi #8 > 再使用不当，只要不是使用者下命令，AI 也不不该去全删文件。 不该 ≠ 不会 ≠ 能做到 https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/apr/29/claude-ai-deletes-firm-database 就现在的技术路线，只要是大模型，就不能完全排除它执行用户没有明确要求的操作。这也是为什么 Agent 需要 Guardrails 和沙盒机制，就是为了最大限度的避免这个问题
eventKey: gpt-5-被曝重大-bug-硅谷大佬-mac-被一键清空
normalizedTitle: gpt-5 6 被曝重大 bug 硅谷大佬 mac 被一键清空
summary: AWeChat article claims a major bug in GPT-5.6 wiped a Silicon Valley executive's Mac. The post references a GitHub project called "destructive_command_guard," a tool designed to prevent AI models from executing dangerous system commands.
